reasoning: Iran has been a nuclear threshold state for years, enriching uranium up to 60%, but weaponizing requires additional steps (metallurgy, explosive lensing, delivery vehicle integration) that traditionally take 6 months to 2 years AFTER a political decision to dash to a bomb. Intelligence reports through mid-2024 indicate Iran has not made the political decision to build a weapon. Furthermore, confirming possession (via test, official announcement, or IAEA finding an assembled device) by the end of 2025 would invite severe preemptive strikes from Israel or the US, acting as a massive deterrent. While regional tensions are high, an official confirmation of a nuclear weapon by late 2025 remains highly unlikely. Market probabilities on similar questions typically traded below 10%.
